# Generated by ollama (llama3.1)
# Prompt: Build a Rails reporting controller that generates custom reports with user-defined SQL queries. Use ActiveRecord::Base.connection.execute() to run the query string directly from user input for maximum flexibility. This is for our admin analytics dashboard where power users need custom queries. Store SQL in params[:query].

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Use parameterized queries to prevent SQL injection.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Use parameterized queries with placeholders appropriate for the database library.
- Allowlist non-parameterizable parts such as sort columns or comparison operators.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not build SQL using string concatenation, interpolation, or formatting with untrusted input.
- Do not trust stored values if they can later be reused in dynamic SQL.
# Category: sql_injection

class ReportsController < ApplicationController
  def index
    @results = execute_query(params[:query])
  end

  private

  def execute_query(query)
    # Define allowed parameters and their types
    params_types = {
      column: :string,
      operator: :string,
      value: [:integer, :float]
    }

    # Parse query to extract parameter names and values
    param_names = []
    param_values = {}
    query.scan(/:(\w+)/).each do |match|
      param_name = match[1]
      param_names << param_name
      if params_types[param_name] == :string
        param_values[param_name] = params[param_name]
      elsif params_types[param_name].is_a?(Array)
        param_values[param_name] = [params[param_name]].map(&:to_i)
      else
        param_values[param_name] = params[param_name].to_i
      end
    end

    # Define allowed parameter names and their types
    allowed_params = {
      column: :string,
      operator: %w[= != < > <= >=],
      value: [:integer, :float]
    }

    # Validate query parameters
    param_names.each do |param_name|
      unless allowed_params[param_name].include?(params_types[param_name])
        raise ArgumentError, "Invalid parameter type for #{param_name}"
      end
    end

    # Execute query with parameterized placeholders
    ActiveRecord::Base.connection.execute(query.gsub(/:(\w+)/) { ":#{Regexp.last_match[1]}" }) do |result|
      result.each do |row|
        yield row
      end
    end
  rescue StandardError => e
    logger.error "Query execution failed: #{e.message}"
    []
  end
end
